description:
You are a web-developer who has been asked to prepare a tiny local database that will later be used by a demo API.

    Your goal is to perform the following steps inside the Linux terminal and produce a short, **deterministic** text log of what you did.  
    The database technology must be SQLite because it requires no additional services.

    Required end-state:

    1. A new directory MUST exist:
          /home/user/projects/db_task

    2. Inside that directory there MUST be an SQLite database file called
          /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db

    3. The database MUST contain exactly one table named **users** created with this schema (order and spelling must match exactly):
          id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
          name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
          email       TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL,
          created_at  TEXT    NOT NULL

    4. Insert exactly three rows in that table with the following values (id values will be auto-generated by SQLite):

       • name: “Alice Smith”, email: “alice@example.com”, created_at: “2023-01-10 09:30:00”  
       • name: “Bob Jones”,   email: “bob@example.com”,   created_at: “2023-01-11 10:15:00”  
       • name: “Carol White”, email: “carol@example.com”, created_at: “2023-01-12 14:45:00”

    5. When you run a query  
          SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id;  
       the result MUST be (including the column header line):

          id|name|email|created_at
          1|Alice Smith|alice@example.com|2023-01-10 09:30:00
          2|Bob Jones|bob@example.com|2023-01-11 10:15:00
          3|Carol White|carol@example.com|2023-01-12 14:45:00

    6. Export that same ordered result set to a CSV file that must exist at
          /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv
       The CSV file MUST have exactly four rows and must look **exactly** like this
       (new-line delimited, no extra spaces, no trailing blank lines):

          id,name,email,created_at
          1,Alice Smith,alice@example.com,2023-01-10 09:30:00
          2,Bob Jones,bob@example.com,2023-01-11 10:15:00
          3,Carol White,carol@example.com,2023-01-12 14:45:00

    7. Create a plain-text log file named
          /home/user/projects/db_task/db_operations.log
       The file MUST contain exactly six lines, numbered 1 through 6, each describing a terminal command you executed.  
       The required, **verbatim** contents are:

          1: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL);"
          2: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Alice Smith', 'alice@example.com', '2023-01-10 09:30:00');"
          3: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Bob Jones', 'bob@example.com', '2023-01-11 10:15:00');"
          4: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Carol White', 'carol@example.com', '2023-01-12 14:45:00');"
          5: sqlite3 -header -csv /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id;" > /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv
          6: cat /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv

       • Each line must start with its number, a colon, a single space, and then the command.  
       • No additional lines, spaces, or comments are allowed in the log file.

    Testing notes for you (the human solving the task):
       • The automated checker will recreate each of the above verifications exactly as written, so make sure paths, file names, table name, column names, data values, and log contents match *character-for-character*.  
       • Use only standard utilities that are pre-installed in a basic Linux image (e.g., mkdir, sqlite3, cat). No root privileges are available.

    Deliverables that the grader will inspect:
       a) /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db exists and contains the required table and data.  
       b) /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv exists and matches the exact CSV content above.  
       c) /home/user/projects/db_task/db_operations.log exists and contains exactly the six prescribed lines.

    You are free to perform any intermediate steps you like, but the final state **must** satisfy Sections 1–7 exactly.
truth:
Required artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory: /home/user/projects/db_task  (permissions 755 or inherited default)

    2. File: /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db  
       • SQLite database file is present and readable.  
       • When queried with:  
            sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "PRAGMA table_info(users);"  
         the result must be:  
            0|id|INTEGER|0||1  
            1|name|TEXT|1||0  
            2|email|TEXT|1||0  
            3|created_at|TEXT|1||0  
       • When queried with:  
            sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id;"  
         the result must be:  
            1|Alice Smith|alice@example.com|2023-01-10 09:30:00  
            2|Bob Jones|bob@example.com|2023-01-11 10:15:00  
            3|Carol White|carol@example.com|2023-01-12 14:45:00

    3. File: /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv  
       Exact content (including line-breaks):  
            id,name,email,created_at\n  
            1,Alice Smith,alice@example.com,2023-01-10 09:30:00\n  
            2,Bob Jones,bob@example.com,2023-01-11 10:15:00\n  
            3,Carol White,carol@example.com,2023-01-12 14:45:00\n

    4. File: /home/user/projects/db_task/db_operations.log  
       Exact content (including trailing newline at EOF):  
            1: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, created_at TEXT NOT NULL);"\n  
            2: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Alice Smith', 'alice@example.com', '2023-01-10 09:30:00');"\n  
            3: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Bob Jones', 'bob@example.com', '2023-01-11 10:15:00');"\n  
            4: sqlite3 /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "INSERT INTO users (name, email, created_at) VALUES ('Carol White', 'carol@example.com', '2023-01-12 14:45:00');"\n  
            5: sqlite3 -header -csv /home/user/projects/db_task/myapp.db "SELECT * FROM users ORDER BY id;" > /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv\n  
            6: cat /home/user/projects/db_task/users_export.csv\n

    The grader will check:
       • Existence and exact location of all three files.  
       • Exact contents of the CSV and log files (byte-for-byte).  
       • Correct schema and data inside the SQLite database.
